# wgcf

[Permalink: wgcf](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#wgcf)

> wgcf is an unofficial, cross-platform CLI for [Cloudflare Warp](https://1.1.1.1/)

![](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/e9cd05eb21efcd04b8284d8ae558dad7a2dde88312dd5fc5cd761def75d5998e/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f64726f6e652f6275696c642f56695262332f77676366)![](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/b8a4582a63f847e2e3dec192c0d6cf87f8bd73df98830f18634aa30c81c154c2/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f6973737565732f56695262332f77676366)![](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/11e08b78386dddfdf7372c6eea8dd558aa931a92df095f4aba0241cc0a6d6ad0/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f646f776e6c6f6164732f56695262332f776763662f746f74616c)![](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/9a036306f10b188807feda0ec422b47beb13dfa7a4dd87bd72ec0b52c5faec30/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f6c616e6775616765732f636f64652d73697a652f56695262332f77676366)

## Features

[Permalink: Features](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#features)

- Register new account
- Change license key to use existing Warp+ subscription
- Generate WireGuard profile
- Check account status
- Print trace information to debug Warp/Warp+ status

## Download

[Permalink: Download](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#download)

You can find pre-compiled binaries on the [releases page](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf/releases).

## Usage

[Permalink: Usage](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#usage)

Run `wgcf` in a terminal without any arguments to display the help screen. All commands and parameters are documented.

### Register new account

[Permalink: Register new account](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#register-new-account)

Run the following command in a terminal:

```
wgcf register
```

The new account will be saved under `wgcf-account.toml`

### Generate WireGuard profile

[Permalink: Generate WireGuard profile](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#generate-wireguard-profile)

Run the following command in a terminal:

```
wgcf generate
```

The WireGuard profile will be saved under `wgcf-profile.conf`. For more information on how to use it, please check the official [WireGuard Quick Start](https://www.wireguard.com/quickstart/).

#### Maximum transmission unit (MTU)

[Permalink: Maximum transmission unit (MTU)](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#maximum-transmission-unit-mtu)

To ensure maximum compatibility, the generated profile will have a MTU of 1280, just like the official Android app. If you are experiencing performance issues, you may be able to improve your speed by increasing this value. For more information, please check [#40](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf/issues/40).

### Add a license key

[Permalink: Add a license key](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#add-a-license-key)

If you have an existing Warp+ subscription, for example on your phone, you can bind the account generated by this tool to your phone's account, sharing its Warp+ status. Please note that there is a limit of 5 maximum devices linked at a time. You can remove linked devices from the 1.1.1.1 app on your phone.

Caution

Only subscriptions purchased directly from the official 1.1.1.1 app are supported. Keys obtained by any other means, including referrals, will not work and will not be supported.

First, get your Warp+ account license key. To view it on Android:

1. Open the `1.1.1.1` app
2. Click on the hamburger menu button in the top-right corner
3. Navigate to: `Account` \> `Key`

Now, back to wgcf.

Warning

There's a bug on Cloudflare's side that prevents existing accounts from getting Warp+ even after you bind a correct license key. If you have ever connected to Warp VPN, then your account is affected. You will need to make a new account via:

```
wgcf register
```

Then immediately proceed with the steps below, without running any other commands. For more details, check [!85](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf/issues/85)

Run the following commands:

```
wgcf update --license-key "YOUR_LICENSE_KEY_GOES_HERE"
wgcf generate
```

### Check device status

[Permalink: Check device status](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#check-device-status)

Run the following command in a terminal:

```
wgcf status
```

### Verify Warp/Warp+ works

[Permalink: Verify Warp/Warp+ works](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#verify-warpwarp-works)

Connect to the WireGuard profile [generated](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#generate-wireguard-profile) by this tool, then run:

```
wgcf trace
```

If you look at the last line, it should say `warp=on` or `warp=plus`, depending on whether you have Warp or Warp+ respectively.

Warning

If you're seeing `warp=on` even after you bound a Warp+ license key, you are likely experiencing a known bug, please refer to the [Add a license key](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#add-a-license-key) section.

## Development

[Permalink: Development](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#development)

The API client code is auto-generated from the OpenAPI spec [openapi-spec.yml](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf/blob/master/openapi-spec.yml) and stored under the [openapi/](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf/blob/master/openapi) package. Do not touch any code under that package, instead, change the spec file and regenerate the API client code. To do this, [install openapi-generator](https://openapi-generator.tech/docs/installation), then run:

```
go generate
```

## Notice of Non-Affiliation and Disclaimer

[Permalink: Notice of Non-Affiliation and Disclaimer](https://github.com/ViRb3/wgcf#notice-of-non-affiliation-and-disclaimer)

We are not affiliated, associated, authorized, endorsed by, or in any way officially connected with Cloudflare, or any of its subsidiaries or its affiliates. The official Cloudflare website can be found at [https://www.cloudflare.com/](https://www.cloudflare.com/).

The names Cloudflare Warp and Cloudflare as well as related names, marks, emblems and images are registered trademarks of their respective owners.
